SELECT.description: Retrieves variable bindings from matched graph patterns, returning a result set of solutions as a table with columns for each projected variable from the WHERE clause patterns.

CONSTRUCT.description: Builds a new RDF graph by substituting matched variable bindings into a triple template, enabling transformation and reshaping of RDF data into different graph structures.

ASK.description: Tests whether a graph pattern has any solution in the dataset, returning a boolean true or false result without retrieving any variable bindings or result rows.

DESCRIBE.description: Returns an RDF graph containing information about specified resources, with the exact triples returned determined by the SPARQL endpoint implementation rather than explicitly specified.

FILTER.description: Restricts query solutions to those where the given boolean expression evaluates to true, supporting comparison operators, regular expressions, string functions, and logical connectives.

OPTIONAL.description: Performs a left outer join by attempting to match an additional graph pattern, retaining solutions even when the optional pattern fails to match for some bindings.

UNION.description: Combines solutions from two or more alternative graph patterns into a single result set, functioning as a logical OR that matches if any of the alternative patterns match.

BIND.description: Assigns the result of an expression to a new variable within a graph pattern, enabling computed values, string concatenation, and arithmetic results to be used in subsequent patterns.

VALUES.signature: VALUES (?var1 ?var2) { (val1 val2) (val3 val4) } | VALUES ?var { val1 val2 }
VALUES.description: Provides inline data bindings that constrain variable values to a specified set, functioning as parameterized queries or lookup tables directly within the SPARQL query body.

GROUP BY.description: Partitions query solutions into groups based on one or more variables or expressions, enabling aggregate functions like COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX over each group.

SERVICE.description: Executes a portion of a query against a remote SPARQL endpoint for federated querying across distributed datasets, with SILENT suppressing errors from unavailable endpoints.

INSERT DATA.description: Adds specified RDF triples to a named graph in the dataset as a SPARQL Update operation, using concrete triple data without variable bindings or pattern matching.

DELETE WHERE.description: Removes all triples matching the specified graph pattern from the dataset in a single SPARQL Update operation, combining the match pattern and deletion template for conciseness.
